title: Production docker-compose.yml reference
description: Provide a production-reference `docker-compose.production.yml` (distinct from TASK-7's dev compose file) that runs the published image (TASK-58) with a persistent volume and documented env vars, ready for a third party to copy and adapt.
acceptanceCriteria: `docker-compose.production.yml` at repo root references the published image (`ghcr.io/<org>/systembook:latest` or a pinned version tag), not a local build context
Defines a named volume for the SQLite data directory, ensuring data survives container recreation/updates
Documents required env vars via `env_file` pointing at a `.env` the operator creates themselves (not committed), matching the variable names from `.env.local`'s placeholders
Includes a healthcheck block using the `/trpc/health.check` endpoint (TASK-5) so orchestrators can detect readiness
Running `docker-compose -f docker-compose.production.yml up -d` against a real pulled image starts a working instance

steps.details: Define a single `app` service using `image: ghcr.io/<org>/systembook:latest`, `env_file: .env`, a named volume for `DATABASE_PATH`'s directory, and a `restart: unless-stopped` policy.

Add `healthcheck: { test: ["CMD", "wget", "-qO-", "http://localhost:$PORT/trpc/health.check"], interval: 30s, timeout: 5s, retries: 3 }` (or equivalent using whatever HTTP client is available in the runtime image).
Add a comment block at the top of the file (or a companion `docs/production-setup.md` section) listing every required env var name, mirroring `.env.local`'s placeholders, without values.

steps.description: Verify against a real pulled image
steps.details: After TASK-58 publishes an image, pull it, create a local `.env` with test values, run `docker-compose -f docker-compose.production.yml up -d`, and confirm the health-check passes and data persists across a `down`/`up` cycle.

technicalNotes: Keep this file clearly named and distinct from the dev compose file (TASK-7) so users don't accidentally run the dev hot-reload setup in production or vice versa.
